data.title: UNI Drops Hard After V-Shaped Rebound Fizzles Amid Mounting Middle East Tension
data.description:
• UNI dropped 6.36% to $7.3864 despite rebounding sharply from a midnight crash, according to CoinDesk Research's technical analysis model.
  • Price recovered 9.5% from an intraday low of $6.82 but lost momentum at $8.40 resistance.
  • Trump warned of “even more brutal” military actions if Iran refuses to make a deal.
  • Price is consolidating near $7.38, with support forming near $7.26 and resistance around $7.50

data.title: Positive U.S. Regulatory Environment More Conducive for Crypto Corporate Activity: JPMorgan
data.description:
• More crypto companies are looking to go public as the U.S. regulatory backdrop improves, JPMorgan said.
  • The bank noted that the number of crypto IPOs this year matches the pace of offerings seen in the bull market of 2021.
  • Venture capital funding is also on the rise, the report said.

data.title: AVAX Plunges 13% as Crypto Sinks on Rise in Mideast Tensions
data.description:
• The cryptocurrency market is highly volatile due following Israel's strikes against Iran, with Avalanche (AVAX) experiencing a 13% correction.
  • AVAX found strong support between $18.57 and $18.70, showing signs of stabilization and potential consolidation.
  • The CoinDesk 20 index has decreased by 6.2% in the past 24 hours, reflecting widespread market impact.

data.title: TON Down 8% After Israeli Strikes Against Iran
data.description:
• Telegram's token, TON, fell 8% in 24 hours, with its price dropping from $3.20 to $2.93.
  • The selloff deepened after Israel's military actions against Iranian facilities and leadership.
  • Despite the decline, TON showed signs of stabilization with a slight recovery to $2.96.

data.title: Brazilian Firm Meliuz's Shares Fall After Planning to Raise $32.4M to Buy Bitcoin
data.description:
• Meliuz raised R$180 million ($32.4 million) through an equity offering to fund its bitcoin acquisition strategy.
  • The move supports Meliuz’s positioning as Brazil’s first public company to adopt bitcoin as part of its treasury reserves.

data.title: Bitcoin Miners Just Had One of Their Best Quarters on Record, JPMorgan Says
data.description:
• JPMorgan said the first three months of 2025 was one of bitcoin miners' best quarters to date.
  • MARA Holdings mined the most bitcoin for the ninth consecutive quarter, the report said.
  • IREN earned the most gross profit for the first time.

data.title: Bitcoin Miner Price Targets Raised to Reflect Improved Industry Economics: JPMorgan
data.description:
• JPMorgan raised its price targets for CleanSpark, Riot Platforms and MARA Holdings.
  • The increases were due to higher bitcoin prices and improving mining profitability, the report said.
  • The bank reiterated its overweight rating on CleanSpark, IREN and Riot Platforms, and its neutral rating for Cipher Mining and MARA Holdings.

data.title: Cardano's Charles Hoskinson Suggests Swapping $100M of ADA for Bitcoin, Stablecoins
data.description:
• Charles Hoskinson proposed converting $100M of ADA into bitcoin and stablecoins (USDM, USDA) to boost Cardano’s DeFi and stablecoin ecosystem.
  • He dismissed concerns over market impact, saying that the token does not have an issue with liquidity.
  • The proposal contrasts with Cardano Foundation CEO Frederik Gregaard’s view that TVL is not a key adoption metric.

data.title: Ether Plunges 7% as Traders Flee to Dollar and Gold After Israel Strikes Iran
data.description:
• ETH dropped 7.05% to $2,536.06 with sharp losses concentrated during U.S. evening trading, according to CoinDesk Research's technical analysis model.
  • Volume reached nearly 692K ETH as price fell from a high of $2,770.56 to $2,477.71.
  • The drop coincided with a global risk-off move following Israeli military strikes in Iran.
  • A modest bounce from the $2,480 area has seen ETH stabilizing in the $2,530 range

data.title: Walmart, Amazon Mull Dollar-Pegged Stablecoins in the U.S.: WSJ
data.description:
• Stablecoins could eliminate costly card processing fees and reduce transaction times, especially for cross-border payments.
  • A stablecoin system from Amazon or Walmart could shift consumer transactions away from banks, intensifying pressure on card networks like Visa and Mastercard.
